title: On Graphs, Probability Theory and Cakes

content.problemStatement:
Chef is viewing a simulation of creating a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) which calculates mysterious coefficients for all the edges. Initially, there is a graph without edges, but there is a set SSS containing N−1N-1N−1 edges (numbered 111 through N−1N-1N−1) which should get added to the graph one by one.

In this process, N−1N-1N−1 times, the following steps are performed:


An edge eee is chosen uniformly randomly from the current set SSS.
This edge is added to the graph and removed from SSS.
The coefficient of eee is set to the number of vertices in the connected component created by adding this edge.
Chef is wondering about the expected values of coefficients of the edges. Unfortunately, he does not have much time for such matters since he needs to prepare a superb cake for Chefina's birthday tomorrow.

Help Chef and for each edge, calculate the expected value of its coefficient modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353. We can prove that each coefficient is a rational number; for a coefficient in the form P/QP/QP/Q, where PPP and QQQ are positive integers and QQQ is coprime with 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, you should compute P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 is the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: the edges in SSS form a tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200,000200,000200,000

With probability 1/21/21/2, the edge (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) is added to the graph first and its coefficient is set to 222 (vertices 111 and 222 are in the connected component). Then, with probability 111, the edge (2,3)(2,3)(2,3) is added and its coefficient is set to 333.
With probability 1/21/21/2, the edge (2,3)(2,3)(2,3) is chosen first instead and its coefficient is set to 222. In this case, the coefficient of (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) is then set to 333.
The expected value of the coefficient of the first edge is 2⋅1/2+3⋅1/2=5/22 \cdot 1/2 + 3 \cdot 1/2 = 5/22⋅1/2+3⋅1/2=5/2. For the second edge, it is the same.
**Example case 2:** The expected values of the coefficients are 17/6,3,17/617/6, 3, 17/617/6,3,17/6.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The following N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges in SSS. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that the iii-th edge connects vertices uuu and vvv.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing N−1N-1N−1 integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be R⋅Q−1R \cdot Q^{-1}R⋅Q−1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353 for the coefficient of the iii-th edge.
